{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks whether whole genome sequencing (WGS) can be used for the diagnosis of mitochondrial disease. The relevant documents from MEDCORP and UMLS are reviewed. In the MEDCORP documents, several references discuss the use of whole-genome sequencing in various genetic disorders. Document [14] specifically mentions that in cases of mitochondrial disorders like Kearns-Sayre Syndrome (KSS), next-generation sequencing (NGS) of mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) is the preferred diagnostic modality. Document [13] notes that whole-exome or whole-genome sequencing may be pursued if no causative variant is identified by panel testing. Document [17] states that if chromosomal microarray or karyotyping fails, exome or whole-genome sequencing may identify causative variants. Document [18] highlights that whole-genome sequencing is expected to play an increasingly significant role in stillbirth evaluation and offers a higher detection rate than comparative genomic analysis. Document [26] and [30] mention whole-exome sequencing revealing mutations in genes associated with Lafora disease, a condition not mitochondrial, but the methodology is relevant. Document [12] explicitly lists whole-genome sequencing as a possible avenue for hereditary ataxias. While mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) is typically sequenced separately, whole-genome sequencing includes both nuclear and mitochondrial DNA. Document [14] notes that mtDNA mutations can be missed in blood due to tissue-specific heteroplasmy, and that sequencing of muscle mtDNA is recommended. Whole-genome sequencing, by covering the entire genome including mtDNA, can detect mutations in both nuclear and mitochondrial genomes. Therefore, WGS is capable of identifying pathogenic variants in mitochondrial diseases, especially when targeted methods fail. The UMLS documents do not provide specific information on WGS for mitochondrial disease, but they support the broader use of genetic testing in neurological and metabolic disorders. Given that WGS includes mtDNA analysis and is used in the evaluation of genetic disorders with mitochondrial components, it is valid for diagnosis. Thus, the evidence supports that whole genome sequencing can be used for diagnosis of mitochondrial disease.", "answer_choice": "A"}
